title: Ghana Card Application Form 1-A (National Identity Card Application Form)
description: Ghana's national form for applying for the national identity card known as the "Ghana Card" — "Form One (regulation 3(1))", "NATIONAL IDENTITY CARD APPLICATION FORM", published by the National Identification Authority (NIA) as "Ghana Card Application Form 1-A" — prescribed under regulation 3(1) of the National Identity Register Regulations, 2012 (L.I. 2111), made under the National Identity Register Act, 2008 (Act 750); the NIA itself is established by the National Identification Authority Act, 2006 (Act 707). Used for first-time issuance, update, replacement, or re-issue of the card (a single applicant-type/request-type checkbox pair at the top of the form) by citizens, permanently resident foreign nationals, persons with a residence permit, and refugees alike. Opens Ghana's National ID & Civic Documents vertical (1 of 6) and the registry's 41st jurisdiction. This schema models the form's own applicant-facing content in full: applicant type/request type, personal particulars (name, sex, marital status, height, eye/hair colour, education level, birth certificate reference, date of birth, nationality at birth and current nationality, place of birth, hometown, occupation, and a full residential address), up to 14 languages spoken, applicant's parentage (father's and mother's full names, nationalities, living status, and home towns), next of kin, an up-to-five-entry spouse list (the form itself directs applicants with more than five spouses to a separate "Spouses Form", out of scope here), a non-citizen-only block (Ghana residence history and employer details), a verification-document reference, a dual-citizenship/naturalization block, local and foreign phone numbers, an email address, six paired institutional-ID/date fields (SSNIT, Voter ID, Passport, National Health Insurance, Driver's Licence, Tax Identification Number), a "Challenged" applicant flag, and the applicant's own declaration (quoted from the form's own text, referencing Section 40 of the National Identity Register Act, 2008, Act 750). It deliberately does NOT model the form's own registration/administrative fields that are filled by NIA staff rather than the applicant — the Interviewer's own NID number, the registration Centre Number, and the "MRW Number" — nor the raw signature/thumbprint capture widget or the pre-printed "Printing Sequence Number" and "Interviewer's Signature" areas (the latter two carry no fillable widget on the source form at all), consistent with this registry's established practice of not treating raw signature/thumbprint capture as a data field. Registering for a Ghana Card requires in-person attendance at an NIA registration centre with supporting identity documentation and biometric capture under the National Identity Register Act, 2008; this schema does not submit the application itself, and the applicant's own NIA registration centre / nia.gov.gh is always authoritative. GovSchema is independent and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or operated by the Republic of Ghana or the National Identification Authority.

source.documentRef: Form One (regulation 3(1) of the National Identity Register Regulations, 2012, L.I. 2111), "National Identity Card Application Form" / "Ghana Card Application Form 1-A"

verification.notes: GovSchema Standard Research cycle. Opens Ghana as this registry's 41st jurisdiction, via its National ID & Civic Documents vertical (1 of 6). This cycle re-fetched and re-derived everything from scratch rather than trusting the prior scouting note: fetched https://nia.gov.gh/wp-content/uploads/Ghana-Card-Application-Form-Form-1-A.pdf directly (HTTP 200, application/pdf, 498,907 bytes, Last-Modified 2026-02-16, sha256:3f401681db2e6d1f39fcc16b78c6b69a71d81a43075789513de29d00593bc5fa; internal PDF metadata: PDFFormatVersion 1.6, Producer 'Corel PDF Engine Version 17.0.0.491', CreationDate 2018-01-24, ModDate 2018-11-27 — i.e. the document's own content dates to 2018 even though the web server re-served it in 2026). Independently confirmed via a fresh fetch of NIA's own current Forms page (https://nia.gov.gh/forms/, HTTP 200) that this exact file is still directly linked today, which is materially stronger provenance than a third-party mirror. The prior scouting note's specific claim of a byte-identical fims.org.gh mirror did NOT reproduce: fims.org.gh is the Foreigners Identity Management System's own site and hosts only a distinct 'Non-Citizen Ghana Card' application form at different paths (Application-Form.pdf / application_form.pdf, both HTTP 200 but a different document); no file at the scouted path exists there (HTTP 404). A genuine independent third-party mirror was found instead (www.gtlegalafrica.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/07/NIA-APPLICATION-FORM.pdf, HTTP 200, 2,046,733 bytes, sha256:d16aa57e615e18fb2fc9857efee3a935dfd85246cd739012874362167654d636) — a flat scanned copy (PDFFormatVersion 1.3, Producer 'IJ Scan Utility', 0 AcroForm widgets confirmed via pdfjs-dist) of the same 2-page 'Form One (regulation 3(1))', corroborating the form's content/layout though not its fillable structure. pdfjs-dist getAnnotations()/getFieldObjects() (own re-derivation) confirms 129 real AcroForm Widget annotations across exactly 2 pages (84 on page 1, 45 on page 2) — IsAcroFormPresent true, IsXFAPresent false — of which 22 are Btn checkboxes/radios forming 6 printed single-select groups (TYPE OF APPLICANT, TYPE OF REQUEST, Marital Status, Level of Education: independent non-grouped checkboxes; Is Father alive / Is Mother alive: genuine radioButton:true pairs), all mapped to their printed labels by correlating each widget's rect against page.getTextContent() items at matching y-coordinate (every field's own internal fieldName IS mostly human-readable on this form, e.g. 'SURNAME', 'FORENAMES', unlike Kenya's Reg. 136A — but several are ambiguous/reused, e.g. 5 repeated Village/Country/District triples and 14 bare numeric boxes '1'-'14', which coordinate correlation was still necessary to resolve). All 129 widgets are accounted for: 103 map 1:1 to a field, 22 (the 6 checkbox/radio groups) consolidate to 6 enum/boolean fields, and 4 are deliberately excluded (Interviewer NID No., Centre Number, and MRW Number — all NIA-staff/administrative fields per their own semantics and position in the form's unnumbered registration header — plus the applicant's own signature/thumbprint capture widget, excluded per this registry's established practice). Two further printed areas (Printing Sequence Number; Interviewer's Signature) carry no widget at all, confirmed independently rather than inferred from their position. Disclosed judgment calls: (1) the five repeated 'Village/Town', 'Region/Country', 'District/State' widget triples (Place of Birth, Hometown, Residential Address, Father's Home Town, Mother's Home Town) are each a single widget carrying two stacked printed labels depending on whether the location is domestic or foreign — modelled as one field per position (e.g. hometownVillageOrTown) rather than inventing two separate fields the source does not provide separate widgets for; (2) nationality-type fields (nationalityAtBirth, currentNationality, and five further nationality fields for parents/spouses/dual-citizenship) are constrained to a 3-character pattern because every one of these widgets' own maxLen is 3 on the source AcroForm — too short for a spelled-out name — modelled as a 3-letter code without asserting the source names a specific standard (ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 is the closest fit); (3) 'sex' is modelled as enum ['M','F'] rather than the spelled-out 'male'/'female' seen on ke/nrb's schema, because this widget's own maxLen is 2, physically too short for a spelled-out word; (4) 'occupation' is a 4-character-max field on the source AcroForm, too short for most occupation titles — disclosed as likely expecting a short occupation code NIA does not publish, not asserted as free text without qualification; (5) one widget in Section 12 (between the Document No./NID field and the Dual Citizenship block) carries no printed label at all — disclosed as verificationDocumentAdditionalReference, following this registry's practice for unlabelled widgets (cf. ke/nrb's undefined 'R.Number' fields, GOV-2500); (6) the four independent TYPE OF APPLICANT/TYPE OF REQUEST/Marital Status/Level of Education checkbox groups are each modelled as a single enum field rather than exploded into independent booleans plus an exclusivityGroups entry, matching this registry's established convention for printed mark-one-of-N checkbox groups (cf. ar/dnrpa/solicitud-tipo-08-transferencia-automotor's civil-status checkbox group) — spec v0.3's exclusivityGroups (§8.4) constrains at most one of a group to be true but does not itself express 'the source form only ever means one', so an enum is the more faithful, more directly validated encoding. requiredWhen is used exactly once (nonCitizenFirstResidenceInGhanaDate, gated on applicantType notEquals 'citizen') — safe because applicantType is itself a required enum field, always present, so this is not the notEquals-against-an-absent-optional-field bug this registry has hit before. Legal-currency check (fresh web search, 2026-07-12): the National Identification Authority Act, 2006 (Act 707) establishes NIA; the National Identity Register Act, 2008 (Act 750) governs the Register and (per its own Section 40, quoted verbatim in the form's declaration) offence/penalty provisions; the National Identity Register Regulations, 2012 (L.I. 2111) prescribe 'Form One' under regulation 3(1) — all three confirmed current via nia.gov.gh/legal-and-regulations/ (live, HTTP 200) with no repeal or replacement found. A separate online registration portal (register.nia.gov.gh, live HTTP 200) also exists as a parallel digital channel, but the paper Form 1-A remains directly linked on NIA's own current Forms page today, so it is not treated as superseded. This cycle also screened Ghana's other five verticals in parallel. DMV and Visa are treated as confirmed dead ends, independently re-confirmed live by this authoring pass (not just carried over from the prior scouting note): DVLA's own portal (https://online.dvla.gov.gh/, HTTP 200) is a login/sign-in-gated individual-registration system with no downloadable specimen form found; evisa.immigration.gov.gh (HTTP 200) is confirmed live as a fully online portal (per the scouting note, Cloudflare-Turnstile + Sumsub-biometric + payment-gated), with visa-on-arrival requiring only a handwritten letter. Passport's backlog candidate (Ghana MFA mfa.gov.gh/wp-content/uploads/2018/10/passport-form.pdf) was independently re-confirmed live this pass (HTTP 200) — a genuine, currently-reachable, text-layer-only specimen, flagged as ready-to-author. Business Formation (RGD/ORC orc.gov.gh/forms-fees/, 'Form 3' for private limited company registration, ~571KB, per the prior scouting note a genuine text layer but 0 AcroForm fields) could not be independently re-confirmed this pass — a direct refetch of orc.gov.gh/forms-fees/ returned a transient HTTP 500 this cycle — so this claim is carried over from the prior scouting note, not independently re-verified, and should be re-checked before authoring. Taxes (GRA DT_0104/DT_0104a provisional self-assessment forms, per the prior scouting note genuine AcroForms with 27/29 fields but a narrower 'provisional estimate' scope than an annual actual return) was likewise not independently re-fetched this pass. All three (Business Formation, Taxes, Passport) are flagged as ready-to-author backlog candidates for a future cycle, not dead ends. See VERIFICATION.md for the full sourcing record, the complete widget-to-label coordinate-correlation table, and the conformance run writeup (2 valid fixtures, 0 errors each; 5 mutation-control fixtures, exactly 1 error each; documents[] requiredness explicitly exercised).

fields.name: applicantType
fields.label: TYPE OF APPLICANT
fields.description: The applicant's category, as ticked on the form. The source prints four independent checkboxes (CITIZEN / PERMANENTLY RESIDENT / PERSON WITH RESIDENCE PERMIT / REFUGEE) intended as a single mark-one-of-four selection, modelled here as one enum field rather than four independent booleans, matching this registry's established convention for printed single-select checkbox groups (cf. ar/dnrpa's civil-status checkbox group).
fields.sourceRef: Section 1 — "TYPE OF APPLICANT *" (checkboxes: CITIZEN / PERMANENTLY RESIDENT / PERSON WITH RESIDENCE PERMIT / REFUGEE)

fields.name: requestType
fields.label: TYPE OF REQUEST
fields.description: The type of request being made, as ticked on the form (four independent checkboxes modelled as one enum, as above).
fields.sourceRef: Section 2 — "TYPE OF REQUEST*" (checkboxes: Issuance / Update / Replacement / Re-Issue)

fields.name: existingNidNumber
fields.label: Existing NID Number
fields.description: The applicant's existing national ID (Ghana Card) number, populated when this application is an update, replacement, or re-issue rather than a first-time issuance.

fields.name: sex
fields.label: Sex
fields.description: The applicant's sex. The printed box accepts at most 2 characters, consistent with a single-letter M/F entry rather than a spelled-out word.

fields.name: maritalStatus
fields.label: Marital Status
fields.description: The applicant's marital status, as ticked on the form (five independent checkboxes — Single / Married / Legally Separated / Divorced / Widowed — modelled as one enum, matching this registry's checkbox-group convention).

fields.name: heightCm
fields.label: Height (cm)
fields.description: The applicant's height in centimetres. The printed box accepts at most 3 digits, so validation.maximum reflects that physical constraint rather than an asserted business rule.

fields.name: disabilityCode
fields.label: Disability Code
fields.description: A short disability code, when applicable. The form prints only "Disability / Code:" with no accompanying code table, so no enum is asserted.

fields.name: levelOfEducation
fields.label: Level of Education
fields.description: The applicant's level of education, as ticked on the form (five independent checkboxes modelled as one enum, as above).
fields.sourceRef: "Level of Education *" (checkboxes: None / Basic / Secondary / Tertiary / Higher)

fields.name: isEstimatedDateOfBirth
fields.label: Date of Birth is Estimated
fields.description: Whether the date of birth given is an estimate rather than a documented date.

fields.name: nationalityAtBirth
fields.label: Nationality at Birth
fields.description: The applicant's nationality at birth. The source form's own box accepts at most 3 characters; modelled as a 3-letter code (ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 is the closest fit, though the form itself does not name a code standard) since a full country/region name would not fit the printed box.

fields.name: currentNationality
fields.label: Current Nationality
fields.description: The applicant's current nationality. The source form's own box accepts at most 3 characters; modelled as a 3-letter code (ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 is the closest fit, though the form itself does not name a code standard) since a full country/region name would not fit the printed box.

fields.name: placeOfBirthVillageOrTown
fields.label: Place of Birth — Village / Town
fields.description: The source prints two stacked labels on one widget — "Village" over "Town" — used for a domestic village name or a foreign town name depending on where the applicant was born/resides.

fields.name: placeOfBirthRegionOrCountry
fields.label: Place of Birth — Region / Country
fields.description: The source prints two stacked labels on one widget — "Region" over "Country" — sized for at most 3 characters (a short region or ISO-alpha-3-style country code), used depending on whether the location is domestic or foreign.

fields.name: placeOfBirthDistrictOrState
fields.label: Place of Birth — District / State
fields.description: The source prints two stacked labels on one widget — "District" over "State" — used depending on whether the location is domestic or foreign.

fields.name: hometownVillageOrTown
fields.label: Hometown — Village / Town
fields.description: The source prints two stacked labels on one widget — "Village" over "Town" — used for a domestic village name or a foreign town name depending on where the applicant was born/resides.

fields.name: hometownRegionOrCountry
fields.label: Hometown — Region / Country
fields.description: The source prints two stacked labels on one widget — "Region" over "Country" — sized for at most 3 characters (a short region or ISO-alpha-3-style country code), used depending on whether the location is domestic or foreign.

fields.name: hometownDistrictOrState
fields.label: Hometown — District / State
fields.description: The source prints two stacked labels on one widget — "District" over "State" — used depending on whether the location is domestic or foreign.

fields.name: occupation
fields.label: Occupation
fields.description: The applicant's occupation. The printed box accepts at most 4 characters — too short for most occupation titles written in full — suggesting NIA expects a short occupation code here rather than free text; the form itself does not publish the code table, so no enum is asserted and the field is modelled as a short string.

fields.label: Residential Address — Village / Town
fields.description: The source prints two stacked labels on one widget — "Village" over "Town" — used for a domestic village name or a foreign town name depending on where the applicant was born/resides.

documents.label: Applicant's declaration
documents.statement: I declare that all the information presented for this application is true and correct and that all documents that I have provided for the purposes of this application are genuine. I understand that if any information I have provided for this application is false or incorrect, I will be liable to prosecution in accordance with Section 40 of the National Identity Register Act, 2008 (Act 750) as well as any other law or regulation which may be in force at the time. I understand that the information and documents I have provided in respect of this application are stored and handled by the NIA and I have the right to have them updated should they change. I declare that all the information contained in this application form has been read, interpreted and explained to me in a language I understand and I perfectly understood and approved same before my hand was guided to make my mark.
documents.sourceRef: Page 2 declaration paragraphs, immediately above "APPLICANT’S SIGNATURE OR THUMBPRINT *"
